title: Grapevine Hairy Root Culture Establishment and Maintenance Protocol
author: Sasha Tietz

steps.title: Streak out Rhizobium rhizogenes onto yeast...
steps.instruction: Streak out Rhizobium rhizogenes onto yeast mannitol (YM) agar plates supplemented with 25 µg/mL Rifampicin and selection antibiotic (if binary vector present). Incubate plates in the dark at 28°C

steps.title: Decant R
steps.instruction: Decant R. rhizogenes culture into 50 mL falcon tubes for processing.

steps.title: Prepare a pre-culture by inoculating 5...
steps.instruction: Prepare a pre-culture by inoculating 5 mL of YM broth with a isolated single R. rhizogenes colony, incubate in the dark at 28°C while shaking at 120 rpm.

steps.title: Use 100-200 µL of pre-culture to...
steps.instruction: Use 100-200 µL of pre-culture to inoculate 50 mL of YM broth in a 100 mL Erlenmeyer flask. Incubate in the dark at 28°C while shaking at 120 rpm until OD 600 = 0.8 - 1.

steps.title: Centrifuge culture at 5000 rpm and decant supernatant
steps.instruction: Centrifuge culture at 5000 rpm and decant supernatant.

steps.title: Wash remaining pellet in 1/2 Murashige...
steps.instruction: Wash remaining pellet in 1/2 Murashige and Skoog (MS) media, centrifuge at 5000 rpm and resuspend in 25 mL 1/2 MS supplemented with 250 µM acetosyringone. The bacterial culture is ready for infection of plant material and can be stored at 4°C for an hour prior to use.

steps.title: Excise internode tissue from about 20cm...
steps.instruction: Excise internode tissue from about 20cm tall grapevine plantlets using sterile scalpel and tweezers.

steps.title: Transfer an active growth point onto...
steps.instruction: Transfer an active growth point onto MS agar in a magenta. Gently push the cut stem side into the media and let the plantlet develop.

steps.title: Decant the bacterial suspension into a...
steps.instruction: Decant the bacterial suspension into a petri dish and immerse the explant material (excised internodes), avoid agitation and completely submerge explants.

steps.title: Remove the explants from the suspension with tweezers
steps.instruction: Remove the explants from the suspension with tweezers. Briefly blot them dry on sterile filter paper to remove excess bacterial suspension but leave them slightly moist.

steps.title: Co-culture the explants and bacteria on...
steps.instruction: Co-culture the explants and bacteria on 1/2 MS agar plates in the dark at 20°C. Bacterial growth should be visible around the explants after 24 - 36 hours.

steps.title: Rinse the explants twice in petri...
steps.instruction: Rinse the explants twice in petri dishes filled with 25 mL autoclaved distilled water.

steps.title: Submerge the explants in a 500...
steps.instruction: Submerge the explants in a 500 µg/mL Timentin/Augmentin antibiotic solution to eliminate R. rhizogenes .

steps.title: Blot the explants dry thoroughly on...
steps.instruction: Blot the explants dry thoroughly on sterile filter paper and transfer them to 1/2 MS agar supplemented with 200 µg/mL Timentin/Augmentin. Incubate in the dark at 25°C.

steps.title: When emerging roots reach 2 -...
steps.instruction: When emerging roots reach 2 - 4 cm in length, excise them and transfer to separate 1/2 MS agar supplemented with 200 µg/mL Timentin/Augmentin. Incubate in the dark at 25°C. Roots that continue growing after excision with a non-geotropic and highly branched phenotype are considered putative hairy roots.

steps.title: Putative hairy roots are flash frozen, pulverized and DNA extracted
steps.instruction: Putative hairy roots are flash frozen, pulverized and DNA extracted. DNA extracts are used in PCR reactions, screening for rol and vir genes to genotypically determine if R. rhizogenes transformation was successful and if the bacterium was eliminated.

steps.title: Hairy roots can be sub-cultured by...
steps.instruction: Hairy roots can be sub-cultured by excising actively growing root tips and placing them on or in fresh 1/2 MS media. This process can be repeated indefinitely.
